mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly higher during periods of FEDFUNDS decline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 300 in CPIAUCSL indicates elevated price pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive monetary policy, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns exceed 1.5% in 40%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 300.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust supply-side expansion that boosts corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO/GDPC1 and SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) above 3.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns exceed 1% in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE > 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals a tightening labor market that increases corporate hiring costs and reduces profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns exceed 0.8% in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) above 103 signals increased manufacturing activity that boosts corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns fall below 0.5% in 70%+ of instances where INDPRO > 103.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) above 1400k signals increased residential construction activity that boosts consumer wealth and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns fall below 1.2% in 60%+ of instances where HOUST > 1400.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL indicate accommodative policy amid inflation, creating uncertainty that suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL and SPX returns exceeds 0.10 over any 2-year window.
